mnemonics.title: Signal Names
mnemonics.content: KILL STOP KEEP: SIGKILL and SIGSTOP cannot be caught; SIGTERM is the polite kill; SIGHUP hangs up terminals; SIGINT interrupts (Ctrl+C); SIGQUIT quit (Ctrl+\)

mnemonics.title: sigaction Flags
mnemonics.content: RESTART QUEUE NODEFER: SA_RESTART restarts syscalls, SA_SIGINFO provides extra info, SA_NODEFER doesn't block signal in handler, SA_NOCLDWAIT stops zombies, SA_ONSTACK uses alternate stack

mnemonics.title: Signal Set Functions
mnemonics.content: EMPTY FILL ADD DEL MEMBER: sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset, sigismember

acronyms.usage: Flag in sigaction that automatically restarts interrupted system calls
Sent to parent when a child process terminates
Function to examine/change the calling process's signal mask
Atomically change mask and wait for signal
Flag that provides additional signal handler arguments (siginfo_t)

visualTips.visualization: Think of signals like phone calls: the kernel is the 'telephone operator' that routes the call (signal) to your process. If you're 'busy' (masked), the call goes to voicemail (pending). When you 'unmask', voicemail is delivered (handler runs).

Picture a signal handler as an urgent meeting that interrupts your work. During the meeting (handler), certain people (signals) may be blocked from disturbing you (sa_mask). After the meeting, you return to work (rest of program).
Think of sigsetjmp as placing a bookmark in a book that remembers not just the page but also your reading environment (signal mask). siglongjmp is like magically returning to that bookmark later.

analogies.analogy: Like a 'Do Not Disturb' mode on your phone. When enabled, calls (signals) don't ring but go to voicemail (become pending). When disabled, you receive all pending notifications.
Like email notifications that pile up when you're in a meeting (signal blocked). When the meeting ends (mask changed), you receive all accumulated emails at once.
Like a security guard who works silently in the background 24/7, never clocking out. They typically ignore most signals except specific ones like shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT).
